Equivalent (JA): システムモデリング言語 (SysML)
Definition (EN): The purpose of this standard is to specify the Systems Modeling Language™ (SysML), to guide the implementation of conformant modeling tools, and to provide the basis for the development of material and other resources to train users in the application of SysML.
Context/Example (EN): SysML is a general-purpose modeling language for modeling systems that is intended to facilitate a model-based systems engineering (MBSE) approach to engineer systems.

Equivalent (JA): モデルベースシステムズエンジニアリング (MBSE)
Definition (EN): SysML is a general-purpose modeling language for modeling systems that is intended to facilitate a model-based systems engineering (MBSE) approach to engineer systems.
Context/Example (EN): It provides the capability to create and visualize models that represent many different aspects of a system.

Definition (EN): (Implicitly defined as an aspect of a system model, representing what a system should do)
Context/Example (EN): This includes representing the requirements, structure, and behavior of the system, and the specification of analysis cases and verification cases used to analyze and verify the system.

Definition (EN): (Implicitly defined as an aspect of a system model, representing how a system is organized)
Context/Example (EN): This includes representing the requirements, structure, and behavior of the system, and the specification of analysis cases and verification cases used to analyze and verify the system.

Definition (EN): (Implicitly defined as an aspect of a system model, representing how a system acts and reacts)
Context/Example (EN): This includes representing the requirements, structure, and behavior of the system, and the specification of analysis cases and verification cases used to analyze and verify the system.

Definition (EN): (Implicitly defined as a type of case used to analyze a system)
Context/Example (EN): This includes representing the requirements, structure, and behavior of the system, and the specification of analysis cases and verification cases used to analyze and verify the system.

Definition (EN): (Implicitly defined as a type of case used to verify a system)
Context/Example (EN): This includes representing the requirements, structure, and behavior of the system, and the specification of analysis cases and verification cases used to analyze and verify the system.

Equivalent (JA): カーネルモデリング言語 (KerML)
Definition (EN): SysML is defined as an extension of the Kernel Modeling Language (KerML), which provides a common, domain-independent language for building semantically rich and interoperable modeling languages.
Context/Example (EN): SysML also provides a capability to provide further language extensions.

Definition (EN): (Implicitly defined as a mechanism to customize SysML for specialized domain-specific applications)
Context/Example (EN): It is anticipated that SysML will be customized using this language extension mechanism to model more specialized domain-specific applications, such as automotive, aerospace, healthcare, and information systems, as well as discipline specific extensions such as safety and reliability.

Definition (EN): Definitions of system and systems engineering can be found in ISO/IEC 15288 Systems and Software Engineering – System Life Cycle Process.
Context/Example (EN): The purpose of this standard is to specify the Systems Modeling Language™ (SysML), to guide the implementation of conformant modeling tools, and to provide the basis for the development of material and other resources to train users in the application of SysML.

Equivalent (JA): システムズエンジニアリング
Definition (EN): Definitions of system and systems engineering can be found in ISO/IEC 15288 Systems and Software Engineering – System Life Cycle Process.
Context/Example (EN): SysML is a general-purpose modeling language for modeling systems that is intended to facilitate a model-based systems engineering (MBSE) approach to engineer systems.
